title: Floral Fluffy Polish Peppers Mousse
description: A light, airy mousse inspired by Polish cuisine and Mediterranean flavors, featuring poached Padron peppers, fresh herbs, and a splash of lemon for brightness.

ingredients.name: Padron peppers
ingredients.notes: about 40 g each, fresh and bright green

instructions: Fill a medium saucepan with water, olive oil, sea salt, and thyme leaves; bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
Add the Padron peppers to the simmering bath and poach for 8–10 minutes, or until skins are blistered and peppers soften.
Remove the peppers with a slotted spoon and transfer to a bowl of ice water to halt cooking; once cool, peel off skins and roughly chop the peppers.
In a blender, combine the poached peppers, heavy cream, Greek yogurt, lemon zest, lemon juice, and honey; blend until completely smooth.
Transfer the pepper mixture to a mixing bowl and use an electric whisk or hand mixer to whip until light, airy, and doubled in volume.
Taste and adjust seasoning with a pinch of salt or more lemon juice if desired.
Divide the mousse into four chilled serving glasses or ramekins and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set.
Just before serving, garnish each mousse with a sprinkle of chopped basil leaves and an extra drizzle of olive oil if desired.
